channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Trump Says Giving EU Until July 4 To Fulfill Trade Deal Or Will Raise Tariffs
item.description: Trump on Friday had threatened to increase tariffs on EU cars and trucks to 25% this week, from 15% currently, because the EU was not complying with the terms of a deal struck in Scotland last July.
item.content:encoded: Trump on Friday had threatened to increase tariffs on EU cars and trucks to 25% this week, from 15% currently, because the EU was not complying with the terms of a deal struck in Scotland last July.

item.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: "Will Knock Them Out A Lot Harder": Trump Threatens More Iran Strikes
item.description: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.

item.title: Two Australian Women Linked To ISIS Charged With Keeping Slave In Syria

media:content.media:description: Both were immediately arrested after their flight landed at Melbourne International airport.

item.description: The pair returned to Australia on Thursday evening for the first time in almost a decade, travelling from a Syrian detention camp where they were stranded after the group's collapse.
item.content:encoded: The pair returned to Australia on Thursday evening for the first time in almost a decade, travelling from a Syrian detention camp where they were stranded after the group's collapse.

item.title: "Very Dynamic President": Trump Praises Meeting With Brazil's Lula At White House

media:content.media:description: Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva met President Trump on Wednesday at the White House.

item.description: US President Donald Trump said that he and Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva discussed many topics, including Trade and, specifically, Tariffs at the White House on Thursday.
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump said that he and Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva discussed many topics, including Trade and, specifically, Tariffs at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Threatens More Iran Strikes If Deal Not Signed "Fast"

media:content.media:description: Trump said that "great damage done" to the Iranian attackers.

item.description: "We'll knock them out a lot harder, and a lot more violently, in the future, if they don't get their Deal signed, FAST!", US President Donald Trump said as the US military carried out strikes on Iranian military targets Thursday.
item.content:encoded: "We'll knock them out a lot harder, and a lot more violently, in the future, if they don't get their Deal signed, FAST!", US President Donald Trump said as the US military carried out strikes on Iranian military targets Thursday.

item.title: US Urges Iran To Free Ailing Nobel Winner Activist Narges Mohammadi

media:content.media:description: Narges Mohammadi is a human rights activist who has spent much of the past two decades behind bars.

item.description: The United States on Thursday urged Iran to free jailed Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi, who is believed to have had two heart attacks in recent weeks.
item.content:encoded: The United States on Thursday urged Iran to free jailed Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi, who is believed to have had two heart attacks in recent weeks.

item.title: US Military Strikes Iran's Qeshm Port, Bandar Abbas: Report
item.description: The US military on Thursday carried out strikes on Iran's Qeshm port and Bandar Abbas, a Fox News reporter said in a post on X, citing a senior US official.
item.content:encoded: The US military on Thursday carried out strikes on Iran's Qeshm port and Bandar Abbas, a Fox News reporter said in a post on X, citing a senior US official.

item.title: US Trade Court Rules Against Trump's 10% Global Tariffs

media:content.media:description: The US trade court on Thursday ruled against President Donald Trump's latest 10% global tariffs.

item.description: The US Court of International Trade ruled in favor of small businesses that challenged the tariffs, which took effect on February 24.
item.content:encoded: The US Court of International Trade ruled in favor of small businesses that challenged the tariffs, which took effect on February 24.

item.title: Kash Patel Is Handing Out Alcohol Bottles To FBI Staff As Gifts: Report

media:content.media:description: Kash Patel is handing out personalised, branded bottles of bourbon featuring his name

item.description: In a new report, the Atlantic stated that Patel is handing out personalised, branded bottles of bourbon featuring his name, personal logo and FBI shield as gifts to staff and civilians, all while a defamation lawsuit against the publication and its r
item.content:encoded: In a new report, the Atlantic stated that Patel is handing out personalised, branded bottles of bourbon featuring his name, personal logo and FBI shield as gifts to staff and civilians, all while a defamation lawsuit against the publication and its r

item.title: US Imposes Sanctions On Iraq's Deputy Oil Minister Over Support To Iran

media:content.media:description: The United States has unilateral sanctions against Iranian oil.

item.description: The deputy minister, Ali Maarij al-Bahadli, "abused his government position to divert Iraqi oil in support of the Iranian regime and its terrorist proxies," State Department spokesman Tommy Pigott said.
item.content:encoded: The deputy minister, Ali Maarij al-Bahadli, "abused his government position to divert Iraqi oil in support of the Iranian regime and its terrorist proxies," State Department spokesman Tommy Pigott said.

item.title: 1,500 Ships Trapped In Gulf Due To Iran's Hormuz Blockade: UN Maritime Body

media:content.media:description: The closure has led to a significant global surge in the price of hydrocarbons.

item.description: Before the conflict's outbreak, a fifth of the world's total petroleum and gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: Before the conflict's outbreak, a fifth of the world's total petroleum and gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Israeli Attack Kills Son Of Hamas Chief In Talks With Trump-Led Board

media:content.media:description: Trump's Gaza plan involves Israeli withdrawal and Hamas disarmament, which remains disputed.

item.description: The attack comes as leaders of Hamas and other Palestinian factions held talks with regional mediators and the Board of Peace's lead envoy this week.
item.content:encoded: The attack comes as leaders of Hamas and other Palestinian factions held talks with regional mediators and the Board of Peace's lead envoy this week.

item.title: Superdry Co-Founder James Holder Sentenced To 8 Years In Jail For Rape

media:content.media:description: Holder was one of the co-founders of Superdry in 2003, but left the group in 2016.

item.description: A jury found Holder, 54, guilty last week of raping a woman in 2022 after meeting her in a bar in Cheltenham.
item.content:encoded: A jury found Holder, 54, guilty last week of raping a woman in 2022 after meeting her in a bar in Cheltenham.

item.title: Chinese Woman Fired Over Husband's Job At Rival Firm, Wins Rs 95 Lakh For "Illegal" Dismissal

media:content.media:description: The court ruled that her dismissal was "illegal and unreasonable."

item.description: Liu denied the allegations, saying she only played a supporting role in the company and had no access to critical trade secrets.
item.content:encoded: Liu denied the allegations, saying she only played a supporting role in the company and had no access to critical trade secrets.

item.title: Man Wielding Weapon Near Ex-Prince Andrew's Home Arrested

media:content.media:description: Andrew was stripped of his royal titles in October by the king.

item.description: Norfolk police confirmed they had been called to an incident late Wednesday near the Sandringham Estate "and the man was arrested on suspicion of a public order offence and possession of an offensive weapon".
item.content:encoded: Norfolk police confirmed they had been called to an incident late Wednesday near the Sandringham Estate "and the man was arrested on suspicion of a public order offence and possession of an offensive weapon".

item.title: ICE Arrests Two Indians For Attempted Lewd Act On Child, $60,000 Theft Case

media:content.media:description: ICE said the weekend crackdown targeted undocumented immigrants with criminal records

item.description: According to the agency, Hemant Khann-Kumar, described as a "criminal illegal alien" from India, had been convicted for an attempted lewd act upon a child. Patel, meanwhile, was for conspiracy to commit theft involving more than $60,000.
item.content:encoded: According to the agency, Hemant Khann-Kumar, described as a "criminal illegal alien" from India, had been convicted for an attempted lewd act upon a child. Patel, meanwhile, was for conspiracy to commit theft involving more than $60,000.

item.title: "More Cases May Appear Soon, But Public Health Risk Low": WHO Chief On Hantavirus Outbreak

media:content.media:description: WHO has implemented several containment measures onboard

item.description: The outbreak occurred on the Dutch-flagged cruise ship MV Hondius, which had travelled from Argentina towards Cabo Verde across the Atlantic.
item.content:encoded: The outbreak occurred on the Dutch-flagged cruise ship MV Hondius, which had travelled from Argentina towards Cabo Verde across the Atlantic.

item.title: Marco Rubio Meets Pope Leo In Bid To Ease Trump-Vatican Tensions

media:content.media:description: Marco Rubio aimed to ease tensions and discuss religious freedom with Vatican officials (File Photo).

item.description: The Trump administration's relations with the Holy See have sharply deteriorated since the election of the first US pope, Leo XIV, on May 8, 2025.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration's relations with the Holy See have sharply deteriorated since the election of the first US pope, Leo XIV, on May 8, 2025.

item.title: Offered To Be "Platonic" Sperm Donor: Shivon Zilis On Relationship With Musk

media:content.media:description: Now, Musk has become "actively" involved in the lives of their four children

item.description: Shivon Zilis said that it first started as a friendship after which Elon Musk reached out to her as a "platonic" sperm donor. In 2020, she decided to have children with Musk after he told her that he would "be happy to make a donation".
item.content:encoded: Shivon Zilis said that it first started as a friendship after which Elon Musk reached out to her as a "platonic" sperm donor. In 2020, she decided to have children with Musk after he told her that he would "be happy to make a donation".

item.title: 2 Women Linked To Islamic State Arrested For Slavery At Australian Airport

media:content.media:description: Widely known as the "ISIS brides", the case has stirred strong feelings in Australia.

item.description: They returned to Australia after allegedly sneaking into Syria to join the Islamic State's self-declared caliphate in the early 2010s.
item.content:encoded: They returned to Australia after allegedly sneaking into Syria to join the Islamic State's self-declared caliphate in the early 2010s.
